common.shortcuts_disabled: Shortcuts are disabled when typing in inputs
common.kiwisdr_consent_p1: KiwiSDR receivers are volunteer-operated by amateur radio operators. Each receiver has a limited number of user slots (usually 4–8) and shares the operator's home internet bandwidth.
common.kiwisdr_consent_p2: Please be respectful: close the popup when you're done listening, and identify yourself with a callsign or handle below so operators know who's connecting.
common.callsign_hint: Shown to the SDR operator in their user list. Leave blank to let KiwiSDR prompt you on first connect.
common.subtitle: GLOBAL THREAT INTERCEPT
common.backend_offline: BACKEND OFFLINE — Cannot reach backend server. Check that the backend container is running and BACKEND_URL is correct.

settings.title: Settings
settings.admin_key_placeholder_locked: Enter operator key for protected settings tabs...
settings.admin_key_placeholder_unlocked: Operator tools unlocked. Enter key only to reseed or recover...

infonet.title: InfoNet
infonet.dashboard_ballot_placeholder: No live ballot counts or policy promises are being advertised in this shell yet.
infonet.dashboard_ballot_note: When governance arrives, it should be real, verifiable, and community-shaped instead of placeholder politics.
infonet.gate_rule_1: 1. FIXED LAUNCH CATALOG: no new gates can be created in this build.
infonet.gate_rule_2: 2. POSTS + REPLIES PERSIST ON THE OBFUSCATED GATE STORE FOR NODES THAT CARRY THIS GATE.
infonet.gate_rule_3: 3. GATE VOTES USE THE EXISTING PUBLIC LEDGER VOTE CONTRACT FOR RECORDKEEPING.
infonet.gate_anon_session_info: Anonymous session is active for this gate. The backend rotates a fresh gate-scoped public key here. You can read, post, reply, and cast the current gate-scoped votes from this room.
infonet.gate_saved_face_info: Saved gate face is active for this room. Posts stay scoped to this gate while the room history persists on the obfuscated gate lane.
infonet.gate_search_placeholder: Search posts or type g/[gate] to jump...
infonet.gate_fixed_launch_notice: Fixed launch gate for the testnet catalog. Dynamic gate creation is disabled.
infonet.gate_obfuscated_notice: Fixed obfuscated gate. Creation is disabled for this testnet.
infonet.shutdown_desc_details: (30-day reversible freeze) → (irreversible archive, 7-day execution delay with one typed appeal allowed). Voting uses oracle_rep_active weight; thresholds are higher for locked gates (75% suspend / 80% shutdown instead of 67% / 75%). Anti-stall: one appeal per shutdown, 48h filing window after vote passes.
infonet.shutdown_suspension_info: During suspension: no gate_message, gate_enter, gate_exit. Members retain membership; content preserved (append-only).
infonet.shutdown_appeal_paused: ⚠ Execution timer is PAUSED while appeal is voted on. If the appeal passes, the shutdown is voided. If it fails, the timer resumes.
infonet.shutdown_irreversible_info: Members released. Content archived. gate_id retired. No petition can reopen.
infonet.shutdown_petition_info: Reason and at least one evidence hash are required. Filing costs common rep (suspend: 15, shutdown: 25, appeal: 20) and triggers a 7-day vote window.
infonet.bootstrap_instead_of: instead of oracle-rep-weighted staking. Eligibility: identity age ≥ 3 days vs market.snapshot.frozen_at, NOT in the predictor exclusion set, and a valid Argon2id PoW (Heavy-Node-only — requires ≥64MB RAM per computation). Once node count crosses
infonet.bootstrap_threshold_desc: (default 1000), new markets default to staked resolution. Existing bootstrap-indexed markets continue under bootstrap rules until they resolve.
infonet.bootstrap_eligibility_info: Eligibility: identity age ≥ 3 days vs market.snapshot.frozen_at, NOT in predictor exclusion set, and a valid Argon2id PoW (Heavy-Node-only). The PoW nonce input is for testnet — production wires the Argon2id solver via privacy-core when the Rust binding lands.
infonet.bootstrap_select_market_hint: Open a bootstrap-indexed market from the Markets view to see its eligible-node-one-vote tally here.
infonet.bootstrap_node_off: Start a local participant node to sync through available Wormhole onion/RNS peers while this backend is running.
infonet.profile_rep_info: Reputation is derived from live lit/dislike activity. Net rep can drop below zero even when the bar is clamped at zero.
infonet.profile_analytics_note: Advanced network-health analytics are not calibrated for this profile yet. Live reputation above is authoritative; unresolved analytics stay zeroed.
infonet.profile_first_contact_desc: Export a signed DM invite for trusted out-of-band exchange. This pins first contact to your messaging identity instead of plain first-sight TOFU. It does not link wallet, reputation, or other personas.
infonet.profile_invite_generated: Signed DM invite generated. Share it only over a trusted out-of-band channel.
infonet.profile_invite_textarea_placeholder: Generate a signed DM invite to display the export blob here.
infonet.profile_qr_invite_desc: Scan this over a trusted out-of-band channel. The QR carries the same signed DM invite shown above, including the trust fingerprint and signature envelope.
infonet.hud_warning: CRITICAL: CROSS-DOMAIN LINKAGE IS PROTOCOL-FORBIDDEN. ROTATING IDENTITY PURGES ALL LOCAL SESSION CACHE.
infonet.market_subtitle: Live Polymarket + Kalshi feeds. Search anything — all markets from both platforms.
infonet.search_placeholder: Search ALL Polymarket + Kalshi markets (e.g. avalanche, bitcoin, trump, war)...
infonet.ballot_not_live: Governance is not live in this shell yet.
infonet.ballot_description: There are no live referendums, petitions, or tallies being advertised here right now.
infonet.no_petitions: No petitions on the chain yet.
infonet.work_description: Earn Credits and Common Rep by fulfilling network contracts.
infonet.exchange_subtitle: Trade crypto assets against Credits. Zero KYC. Zero logs.
infonet.scan_qr: Scan QR code or copy address below
infonet.send_only_warning: Send ONLY {name} ({symbol}) to this address. Sending any other asset will result in permanent loss.
infonet.terminal_description: Experimental operator deck for encrypted gates, mesh comms, dossiers, prediction markets, and live intel routing.
infonet.commands_type_or_click: COMMANDS · type or click to launch
infonet.participant_node_desc: Backend bootstrap is configured; the participant node syncs the testnet seed over the private seed lane.
infonet.private_seed_lane_desc: Participant-node bootstrap, sync, and public chain hosting use the backend private seed lane.
infonet.private_seed_lane_desc2: Turn Wormhole on for gates, obfuscated inbox, and the stronger obfuscated lane only.
infonet.type_to_post: TYPE TO POST · / FOR COMMANDS · CLEAR KEEPS YOUR GATE OPEN
infonet.enter_to_execute: ENTER TO EXECUTE · TYPE CLEAR TO WIPE OUTPUT
infonet.type_clear_info: type clear to wipe output · gates require wormhole · mesh stays public
infonet.private_lane_prompt_enter: Obfuscated lane detected. Enter Wormhole now to sync into the Infonet Commons and communicate through gates.
infonet.private_lane_prompt_activate: No obfuscated lane is active yet. Activate Wormhole now and enter the Infonet Commons?
infonet.wormhole_desc_1: Wormhole turns on the obfuscated lane for gates and the obfuscated commons.
infonet.wormhole_desc_2: If a Wormhole identity already exists, it is reused. If one does not exist yet, it is bootstrapped once.
infonet.wormhole_desc_3: Participant-node sync and public chain hosting stay on the backend node lane.
infonet.wormhole_desc_4: Public mesh stays public and separate.
infonet.gate_prompt_body: Gates live behind Wormhole in this build. Enter now?
infonet.gate_prompt_ready: Yes takes you straight into the gates.
infonet.gate_prompt_not_ready: Yes turns on Wormhole and provisions an obfuscated lane identity, then sends you into gates.
infonet.gate_denied_msg: Gate access denied. Infonet Commons gates remain restricted.
infonet.mesh_stays_public_msg: Mesh stays public. Type 'gates' or tap ENTER WORMHOLE later when you want the obfuscated commons.
infonet.staying_public_msg: Staying on the public lane for now.
infonet.gate_enter_comes_from: Gates live behind Wormhole. Enter now?

timeline.title: Time Machine
timeline.waiting_desc: Recording is on. Playback controls will appear after the first capture.
timeline.enable_snapshots_desc: Enable snapshots to record map state and play it back later.

onboarding.step1_desc: 60+ live data layers in one unified interface
onboarding.read_only_desc: Can see live telemetry but cannot change anything
onboarding.full_access_desc: Can place pins, create layers, and trigger display actions

radio.mesh_notice_desc: These Meshtastic messages are public/degraded, not private. They may be intercepted, relayed, logged, or fail to deliver.
radio.ack_label: I understand this message is public and not private.

sar.title: SAR Ground-Change Detection
sar.no_aois_yet: No AOIs defined yet. Click "Add AOI" to create one.
sar.click_map_place: CLICK THE MAP TO PLACE AOI CENTER
sar.chooser_intro: SAR (synthetic aperture radar) detects ground changes through cloud cover, at night, anywhere on Earth. ShadowBroker offers two modes — both free. Pick one.
sar.mode_a_desc: Free Sentinel-1 scene metadata from Alaska Satellite Facility. No account, no downloads, no credentials. Tells you when radar passes happened over your AOIs and when the next pass is coming.
sar.mode_a_confirm: Mode A is fine — don’t ask again
sar.mode_b_desc: Adds pre-computed anomalies from NASA OPERA, Copernicus EGMS, GFM, EMS, and UNOSAT. Requires a free NASA Earthdata account (~1 minute).
sar.stored_locally: Stored locally on this node only. Never shared. Revoke anytime in Settings → SAR.
sar.token_too_short: Earthdata token looks too short. Paste the full token string.
sar.activate_failed: Failed to activate Mode B. Check the backend logs.

changelog.title: WHAT'S NEW
changelog.required_opensky: Required: OpenSky API credentials for airplane telemetry

startup.title: STARTUP CACHE
startup.subtitle: FIRST RUN WARMUP
startup.desc: The first launch builds local caches for flights, ships, satellites, CCTV, fires, and threat intelligence. Cached launches paint the map much faster; a brand-new install can take a few minutes while upstream feeds are synthesized.

popups.iss_feed_desc: Earth view from ISS external cameras • Dark = nightside pass
popups.aprs_receive_only: APRS is receive-only — transmitting requires a ham radio license
popups.hypothesis_generator: HYPOTHESIS GENERATOR — NOT A VERDICT. This is a signal for further investigation.
popups.placed_by_agent: Placed by OpenClaw agent — click trash icon to remove
popups.proxy_and_direct_failed: proxy and direct source both failed
popups.callsign_hint: Shown to the SDR operator in their user list. Leave blank to let KiwiSDR prompt you on first connect.
popups.consent_intro_p1: KiwiSDR receivers are volunteer-operated by amateur radio operators. Each receiver has a limited number of user slots (usually 4–8) and shares the operator's home internet bandwidth.
popups.consent_intro_p2: Please be respectful: close the popup when you're done listening, and identify yourself with a callsign or handle below so operators know who's connecting.

sentinel_modal.title: SENTINEL HUB IMAGERY
sentinel_modal.intro: You now have access to ESA Sentinel-2 satellite imagery directly on the map. This uses the Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem with your own credentials.
sentinel_modal.true_color_desc: Natural RGB — see terrain, cities, water
sentinel_modal.how_it_works_1: Sentinel-2 revisits every ~5 days — not every location has data every day
sentinel_modal.how_it_works_2: The date slider picks the end of a time window; zoomed out uses wider windows
sentinel_modal.how_it_works_3: Black patches = no satellite pass on that date range (normal)
sentinel_modal.how_it_works_4: Best results at zoom 8-14 — closer = sharper imagery (10m resolution)
sentinel_modal.how_it_works_5: Cloud filter auto-skips tiles with > 30% cloud cover
